script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Good morning, Japan! And a very warm welcome to everyone joining us on The Daily Tanu-chan Show this Thursday, June 18, 2026! I'm Alex!
script.ja: 皆さん、おはようございます！2026年6月18日木曜日、「The Daily Tanu-chan Show」へようこそ！Alexです！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: And I'm Sarah! Alex, you're looking a little... damp this morning. Did you forget your umbrella?
script.ja: そしてSarahです！Alex、今朝は少し…しっとりしていますね。傘を忘れましたか？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Damp is an understatement, Sarah! It's absolutely pouring out there! My hair has already given up on the day, and we've barely started!
script.ja: しっとりどころか、Sarah！外は土砂降りですよ！まだ始まったばかりなのに、僕の髪は今日一日を諦めました！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Well, you're not wrong. Tokyo is experiencing moderate rain today, with a 100% chance of showers. The high will be around 24.6 degrees Celsius, and the low 19.6 degrees.
script.ja: ええ、おっしゃる通りですね。今日の東京は並の雨で、降水確率は100%です。最高気温は24.6度、最低気温は19.6度になるでしょう。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: So basically, a perfect day to stay inside, drink some hot tea, and listen to us on The Daily Tanu-chan Show, right?
script.ja: つまり、家で温かいお茶を飲みながら、「The Daily Tanu-chan Show」を聴くのにぴったりの日ってことですね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly! Or, if you must go out, make sure you have a strong umbrella and good rain boots. Don't let the rain dampen your spirits, Alex.
script.ja: その通りです！もしくは、外出するなら丈夫な傘と良い長靴を忘れずに。Alex、雨に気分を湿らせられないでくださいね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I'll try! Speaking of dampening spirits, let's look at what happened 'On This Day' in history. Sarah, what do you have for us?
script.ja: 頑張ります！気分を湿らせる話といえば、歴史上の「この日」に何が起こったか見てみましょう。Sarah、教えてください。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Today, June 18th, back in 2007, a terrible event occurred in Charleston, South Carolina, USA. The Charleston Sofa Super Store fire broke out, and tragically, nine firefighters lost their lives battling the blaze.
script.ja: 今日、6月18日は、2007年にアメリカのサウスカロライナ州チャールストンで恐ろしい出来事が起こりました。チャールストンソファスーパーストアで火災が発生し、悲劇的なことに、9人の消防士が火と戦い命を落としました。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Wow, that's incredibly sad. Nine brave individuals, just doing their job to protect others. It really makes you appreciate the sacrifices firefighters make every day.
script.ja: それは信じられないほど悲しいですね。9人の勇敢な人々が、ただ他人を守るために仕事をしていて。消防士が毎日払う犠牲に本当に感謝します。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Absolutely. It's a somber reminder of the dangers they face. We send our thoughts to their families and colleagues.
script.ja: 本当にその通りです。彼らが直面する危険を厳粛に思い出させますね。彼らの家族や同僚に思いを馳せます。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Indeed. A moment of silence for those heroes. And now, let's move to something important for families. We have a parenting tip from the organization Zero to Three.
script.ja: ええ。その英雄たちに黙祷を。さて、次は家族にとって大切なことです。Zero to Threeという団体からの子育てのヒントです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, they've released a new report about the well-being of babies and families. It highlights 'economic and care disparities' across the nation.
script.ja: はい、彼らは赤ちゃんと家族の幸福に関する新しい報告書を発表しました。国内全体の「経済的およびケアにおける格差」を強調しています。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Economic and care... 'disparities'? That's an interesting word. What exactly does 'disparities' mean, Sarah?
script.ja: 経済的、ケアにおける…「disparities」（格差）？面白い言葉ですね。「disparities」って具体的にどういう意味ですか、Sarah？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Great question, Alex! 'Disparities' means significant differences or inequalities, especially between groups of people. So, in this context, it's about the unequal access to money, resources, and childcare that different families experience.
script.ja: 良い質問ですね、Alex！「Disparities」とは、特に人々のグループ間の著しい違いや不平等を意味します。この文脈では、異なる家族が経験するお金、資源、育児への不平等なアクセスについてです。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, I see. So, unfair differences in what families have and what care they can get. The report says 41% of low-income families saw their earnings decline in 2021, and childcare is still a big challenge.
script.ja: なるほど。つまり、家族が持っているものや得られるケアに不公平な違いがあるということですね。報告書によると、2021年には低所得世帯の41%が収入減を経験し、育児は依然として大きな課題だそうです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's right. And it also states that the mental and physical health of infants and toddlers continues to be 'jeopardized' by the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic.
script.ja: その通りです。また、乳幼児の精神的・身体的健康が、COVID-19パンデミックの継続的な影響によって「jeopardized」（危険にさらされている）と述べられています。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: 'Jeopardized'? That sounds quite serious. Does it mean like... put in danger?
script.ja: 「Jeopardized」（危険にさらされている）？かなり深刻に聞こえますね。それは…危険に陥っている、という意味ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly, Alex! To 'jeopardize' means to put something at risk or in danger. So, the report is saying that the health of these young children is still very much at risk due to these difficult situations.
script.ja: その通り、Alex！「Jeopardize」とは、何かを危険にさらしたり、リスクに置いたりすることです。つまり、この報告書は、これらの困難な状況のために、幼い子供たちの健康が依然として非常に危険にさらされていると述べているのです。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's heartbreaking. It's a tough world for many families right now. We need to remember to support each other and look out for the little ones.
script.ja: それは胸が痛みますね。今、多くの家族にとって大変な世の中です。お互いを支え、子供たちを見守ることを忘れてはいけませんね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Absolutely. It really highlights the importance of strong community support and good policies to help families thrive. Every baby deserves a strong start.
script.ja: まったくその通りです。家族が繁栄するための強力な地域社会の支援と良い政策の重要性を浮き彫りにしていますね。すべての赤ちゃんが力強いスタートを切るに値します。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Well said, Sarah. On a slightly lighter note, did you hear about the two peanuts who walked into a bar?
script.ja: よく言いました、Sarah。少し軽い話になりますが、バーに入った2つのピーナッツの話を聞きましたか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: (Sighs playfully) Oh, Alex. Here we go. What happened?
script.ja: （楽しそうにため息をつきながら）ああ、Alex。始まりましたね。それでどうしたんですか？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: One was a-salted!
script.ja: 一方が、a-salted（襲われた／塩を振られた）！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: (Laughs) Okay, okay, that was actually pretty good! You got me there. A-salted. I like it.
script.ja: （笑いながら）はい、はい、それは意外と良かったですよ！やられました。a-salted。気に入りました。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: See? My puns aren't always *terrible*!
script.ja: ね？僕のダジャレはいつもは*ひどい*わけじゃないんですよ！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They're... improving! And that's all the time we have for today on The Daily Tanu-chan Show. Thank you for joining us.
script.ja: 改善されていますね！そして、今日の「The Daily Tanu-chan Show」はここまでです。ご視聴いただきありがとうございました。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yes, thank you for listening! Stay dry, stay safe, and we'll talk to you again tomorrow. Bye for now!
script.ja: はい、聴いてくださってありがとうございます！濡れないように、安全に、また明日お会いしましょう。またね！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Bye, everyone!
script.ja: 皆さん、さようなら！

wikipedia.text: The Charleston Sofa Super Store fire kills nine firefighters in Charleston, South Carolina.

parenting.title: New Report Reveals Economic and Care Disparities, Continued Mental and Emotional Distress Among Nation’s Babies and Families
parenting.summary: ZERO TO THREE report shows 41% of low-income families saw earnings decline in 2021, and that access to childcare remains a challenge The mental and physical health of our nation’s infants and toddlers continues to be jeopardized by the devastating impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic. The fourth annual State of Babies Yearbook: 2022, released today by leading early childhood nonprofit ZERO TO THREE
